title: Convention-articulation enforcement — the validator requires planning artifacts to cite a governing convention or a missing-convention gap
description: context-validate requires every decision, feature, and task to articulate its governance: an item_governed_by_convention edge to a convention, OR an item_acknowledges_missing_convention edge to a 'no convention governs X' framework-gap. A planning artifact filed with neither is a validation violation, so conventions cannot be silently skipped and a missing convention surfaces as tracked debt rather than a silent opt-out.
motivation: Conventions govern planning before implementation, but their composition into context relies on the LLM reading and heeding them. Enforcing articulation at validation makes governance a checked precondition, not an aspiration.
acceptance_criteria: [orchestrator] A decision/feature/task with neither an item_governed_by_convention nor an item_acknowledges_missing_convention edge is flagged by context-validate.
[orchestrator] An item edged to a convention, or to a missing-convention gap, passes.
[subagent] The mechanism is config-only — two relation_types + three requires-edge invariants — with no validator code change.
[orchestrator] .context enforces at severity error (clean after backfill); the shipped catalog enforces at severity warning for fresh substrates.
